div1.entryFree: Beck, subst., significant nod, as a sign of command: thy b. might from the bidding of the gods command me, Ant. III, 11, 60. serving of --s and juttingout of bums, Tim. I, 2, 237 (i. e. servile attention to becks). At a person's b. == at command: being at your b. Sonn. 58, 5. ready at thy b. Shr. Ind. 2, 36. they have troops of soldiers at their b. H6C I, 1, 68. with more offences at my b. than I have thoughts to put them in, Hml. III, 1, 127.
